summary: Excellent audience recording of a July 17‑1989 Alpine Valley show highlighted by a blazing “Standing on the Moon” and a memorable Dark Star.
review: Fans rave about the warm, vivid atmosphere captured on this audience tape – it sounds “awesome” and delivers the energy of a classic late‑summer Dead run. The second set shines with a scorching “Standing on the Moon” and an inspiring Dark Star, while the first set is solid but less remarkable. Minor recording quirks (a flat mix and a drum/space cut) don’t detract from overall enjoyment.

ai_rating.rationale: All substantive reviews are 4‑5 stars, praising the performance (especially the second set) and noting only modest recording imperfections.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, lively audience tape with a slightly flat mix reminiscent of a soundboard; one reviewer notes a cut in the drums/space segment but overall the tape is listenable and captures the crowd vibe.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as solid and standard; second set is the real shiner, building from a blazing “Standing on the Moon” into extended improvisations.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his tone and stage presence (rare red pocket tee, lovely playing).
